# **PATTERNS-INDEX**

**Last restructured:** 2 May 2026 as part of the memory architecture migration.  
This document is the entry point. All patterns moved to 5 thematic files. The original PATTERN content is now distributed across the files below. This doc retains its original ID so existing references continue to resolve.

## **File Map**

• **PATTERNS-OPERATIONS** (1DNU3wTPWvY8AoPGfk0YRCKcgh3phIEBQ-Z7331Vj8PI) \- daily workflow patterns

## **Notes on Pattern Numbering**

Pattern 19 had three variants in the legacy library: a Python brace-matcher (dropped, superseded by Pattern 22 Acorn AST), a Website Build Recipe (preserved as Pattern 29 in PATTERNS-WEBAPPS), and the Session Checkpoint Routine (locked 2 May 2026, the canonical Pattern 19 in PATTERNS-OPERATIONS).  
Pattern 18 had three variants: an early Impressum/Imprint pattern (preserved as Pattern 28 in PATTERNS-WEBAPPS), the Modal Variant \+ Nurdug refinement (folded into Pattern 28), and the Strategic Report DOCX Standard (locked 2 May 2026, the canonical Pattern 18 in PATTERNS-DOCUMENTS).  
Pattern 22 had two variants: Proactive Audit-Mode and Acorn AST-Walking. Both kept and merged under Pattern 22 in PATTERNS-OPERATIONS, since Acorn explicitly supersedes the older Pattern 19 brace-matcher and the audit-mode methodology is complementary.  
Total patterns indexed: 29  
\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

## **How to Use**

1\. Look up a pattern by number above to find which file it lives in.  
2\. Open the corresponding PATTERNS-\* doc and search for Pattern N: to find the full content.  
3\. Memory entries that reference "PATTERNS-LIBRARY" still resolve here. Updated memory entries will reference the specific PATTERNS-\* file directly.

## **Adding New Patterns**

New patterns should be added to the appropriate thematic file (not this index) and then registered here in the Pattern Index section. Numbers increment monotonically across the entire library to avoid future collisions.

\#\# Index Update \- 15 May 2026

Pattern 37 added to PATTERNS-STRATEGIC: Corrective Discipline \- Strategy Critic, Not Stenographer. Codified after the 15 May 2026 corpus synthesis session (twelve 14 May INGEST documents plus four 15 May manual ingests covering Seaman, Baunov-Chadayev, Pinto-Gindin, and the German Iran-China oil paper verification update). The synthesis drafted observations on current strategy directions in descriptive framing rather than corrective framing. Michael Herzog corrected: "Our job is to correct wrong strategies or propose better ones." The pattern locks in the discipline before the active Hegemony Series drafting window (Doc IIIb post-summit, Doc IV pre-draft pickup, Doc V synthesis note).

\#\# Index Update \- 17 May 2026  
Pattern 38 added to PATTERNS-STRATEGIC: Brief-Embedded Conclusion \- Reframe Before Researching. Codified at the close of the 16-17 May 2026 Doc VII research / Doc V drafting session in response to Michael Herzog's request to perpetuate the session working posture for later SAD sessions. Pattern 38 is the third anti-absorption sibling: Pattern 36 prevents anchoring to analyst consensus, Pattern 37 prevents anchoring to current-strategy direction, Pattern 38 prevents anchoring to the conclusion embedded in the requester own instruction. It carries a regeneration note recording that the transferable form of the working posture is the body of fact-specific guardrails plus Patterns 36/37/38 read together at session start, not a personality instruction. Trigger: any SAD research or drafting request whose framing asserts its own conclusion, bundles mutually hostile actors into one bloc, treats a demographic category as a unified actor, or asks for evidence-building toward a held position. Application is a six-step reframe-before-research procedure; the reframe is stated to the requester, not performed silently, and holds under pressure. Lookup: Pattern 38, Brief-Embedded Conclusion \- Reframe Before Researching, lives in PATTERNS-STRATEGIC. Total patterns indexed as of 17 May 2026: 38\.

Relationship to existing patterns: Pattern 37 is the sister of Pattern 36\. Pattern 36 prevents anchoring to consensus surface-reading; Pattern 37 prevents anchoring to current-strategy direction. Both are anti-absorption disciplines applied at different stages of analysis. Pattern 37 should be triggered on every paragraph in SAD output that touches a current or proposed strategy by an allied or partner government. Application is a four-question test: is the strategy right or wrong, what is the error, what is the better alternative, and who is the audience that can act on the correction.
